title: The Doom Proving
covenant: A drowned border ford where the salvage of a broken caravan must be dragged home before the marsh takes it back, and every howl in the fog is closer than the last.
styleBible: Romantic dark-fantasy oil painting with gold-leaf light, deep atmospheric perspective, expressive faces, and restrained PG-13 peril.

hero.name: Halvard
hero.bearing: A broad-built march-warden in scarred half-plate, grey-streaked beard cropped short, a round shield slung across his back.
hero.background: A ford-keeper who has buried more friends than enemies and means to bury no more.

narration_blocks.text: The Reed-Water Ford lies grey under first light, the broken caravan heeled over in the shallows like a drowned ox. Fog walks the reed-line and does not lift.

narration_blocks.speaker: Vessarine Olt
narration_blocks.text: Salvage first, mourning after. The marsh keeps what dawdlers leave.

cast_add.name: Vessarine Olt
cast_add.visual: A wiry salvage-mistress in oiled leathers, a silver-shot braid over one shoulder, and a cracked brass spyglass on a neck-cord.
cast_add.voice: Dry and clipped, kind only when it costs nothing.
cast_add.goal: Bring the caravan's salvage and every living hand home.
cast_add.secret: She promised the dead teamster she would carry his lantern to Havenwood.

cast_add.name: Brannoc
cast_add.visual: A young sledge-hand with rope-scarred palms, a patched fen-cloak, and a fisher's long knife at his belt.
cast_add.voice: Quick and eager, a little too loud in the fog.
cast_add.goal: Prove he can bring a load home the way his father did.
cast_add.secret: He signed the salvage debt in his own name.

cast_add.name: Sela
cast_add.visual: A quiet ford-scout with reed-woven bracers, crop-cut dark hair, and a sling of throwing irons.
cast_add.voice: Low and spare, every word weighed before it is spent.
cast_add.goal: Read the water before the water reads them.
cast_add.secret: She has heard the howlers before and never told anyone.

turns.player: We load the salvage sledge and take the ford road.

narration_blocks.text: Rope creaks and the sledge takes the weight. Brannoc sets his shoulder to the frame while Vessarine counts the crates twice and trusts neither count.

narration_blocks.speaker: Brannoc
narration_blocks.text: Get the salvage home — that is the whole of the debt, and I mean to pay it.
Sela slips into the reeds without a sound. Vessarine presses a fisher's blade into Brannoc's hand and shows him the grip her father showed her.

narration_blocks.speaker: Sela
narration_blocks.text: Tracks in the mud. Big. Keeping pace with us on the blind side of the fog.

turns.player: We push past the drowned mile-stone before dark.

narration_blocks.text: They find Vessarine's watch-fire cold at the mile-stone, her spyglass set neatly on a flat stone, facing home. The marsh gives back nothing else of her.

narration_blocks.speaker: Sela
narration_blocks.text: Marsh howlers. That is what took her. Lean grey shapes — I saw one at dusk, yellow eyes over the reed-tops.

creature_add.visual: a lean marsh-wolf with slate-grey hide, moss-matted shoulders, a bone-white blaze down the muzzle, and yellow lamplike eyes
creature_add.nature: a pack hunter of the reed-flats that circles wounded prey and calls the fog down

cast_update.name: Vessarine Olt
cast_update.last_seen: Took the night watch at the mile-stone and did not come back.

turns.player: Howls — form up around the sledge!

narration_blocks.text: The fog folds open and the pack is simply there: three lean shapes, low over the reed-water, circling the sledge in a slow and narrowing noose.

turns.player: Hold the line at the sledge!

narration_blocks.text: Steel and spray. One howler goes down under Halvard's shield-edge; a second breaks and is swallowed whole by the fog. The third drags Brannoc off his feet at the water-line, and he does not get up.

npc_actions.action: Circles the downed sledge-hand, hackles up, waiting.
